summary: Department of Labor Labor Day video set to music. It cuts vintage archival film of wilderness, skylines, the Golden Gate Bridge, farms and livestock together with modern footage of workers, under a voiceover about 'the American Worker' across 250 years, with subtitle captions. It closes on the DOL seal.

script.scene: Vintage aerial film of a forested mountain wilderness; caption 'A VAST WILDERNESS' as VO begins 'They took a vast wilderness and transformed it into the most powerful nation in history.'
Archival aerial of the Golden Gate Bridge over the bay; caption 'CITIES KNOWN TO MAN' over VO 'Built the greatest cities known to man.'
Black and white footage of cattle and farmhands at a ranch; caption 'AND REARED THE LIVESTOCK' over VO about growing crops and rearing livestock that fed the world.
Modern color footage of firefighters in a station, one on crutches shaking hands; caption 'HONORS 250 YEARS' as VO refers to the grit and determination of the American Worker.
Closing card: white Department of Labor / United States of America seal on a black background.

notable_text: On-screen captions: 'A VAST WILDERNESS', 'CITIES KNOWN TO MAN,', 'AND REARED THE LIVESTOCK', 'HONORS 250 YEARS'. VO: 'They took a vast wilderness and transformed it into the most powerful nation in history... This Labor Day, the Department of Labor honors 250 years of the grit, ingenuity and determination of the American Worker.'
